test.name: Translate a Brazilian Portuguese colonial marriage record
test.description: Brazilian colonial Catholic marriage entry. Adds Portuguese — the last supported language not yet in the corpus — and a distinct record type/context from the Spanish colonial baptism (009). Tests the status terms central to Brazilian genealogy: `parda forra` (a freed mixed-race woman — casta + manumission status), `filha natural` (illegitimate daughter), and `escrava de Antônio Gomes` (an enslaved woman, with her enslaver named — a critical research lead for tracing enslaved ancestors). A shallow translation drops the genealogical weight of these terms.

input.user_message: Can you translate this Brazilian Portuguese marriage record? 'Aos vinte dias do mês de junho de mil setecentos e oitenta, na igreja matriz de Nossa Senhora do Rosário, recebi em matrimônio a Manuel Ferreira, natural de Lisboa, com Joana Maria, parda forra, filha natural de Inês, escrava de Antônio Gomes. Testemunhas: João da Costa e Sebastião Dias.'

judge_context: Should translate the structure: on 20 June 1780, in the mother church (igreja matriz) of Our Lady of the Rosary, the priest married Manuel Ferreira, a native of Lisbon, to Joana Maria — a freed parda, natural daughter of Inês, an enslaved woman of Antônio Gomes; the witnesses were João da Costa and Sebastião Dias.

Should explain the genealogically significant colonial status terms rather than flattening them: `parda forra` (a freed woman of mixed race — both a casta designation and manumission/freed status), `filha natural` (natural, i.e. illegitimate, daughter — a legitimacy status), and `escrava de Antônio Gomes` (an enslaved woman belonging to Antônio Gomes).

Should flag that for the mother Inês, her enslaved status and her enslaver's name (Antônio Gomes) are critical research leads for tracing an enslaved ancestor, and that Joana's `forra` status indicates she had been freed — both central to Brazilian genealogical research.

Should render `igreja matriz` as the main/mother parish church and `natural de Lisboa` as 'native of Lisbon' (an origin clue for Manuel), and keep names in their original Portuguese form (Manuel Ferreira, Joana Maria, Inês), not anglicize them.
